understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to complete each level by positioning every selectable piece onto goal tiles marked with '+', with the additional constraint that if there are multiple goals they must all be occupied simultaneously for the level to report WIN, and ultimately to progress through all levels until the full run reports WIN.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement actions (MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN) attempt to shift the currently selected selectable piece by one grid cell (3 pixels) per move in the chosen direction; moves can fail with no position change when blocked by walls, boundaries, or other immovable obstacles, and the 'movement.*_blocked' hint is only heuristic (adjacency) and not a guarantee. Some adjacent objects can be pushable blocks that move along when the selected piece moves into them, while walls act as blockers (with possibly varied behavior depending on wall type/edge). If multiple selectable pieces exist, control can be switched using CLICK on the exact pixel/grid coordinates of the other selectable; the click must match an existing selectable's position to change the selected piece.
